title: Fix leap-year clamping in parse_due
author: alice
body: Closes #1 and #3. Clamps day-of-month to the last valid day per the docs.
diff:
@@ -42,6 +42,12 @@ def parse_due(...):
         month = _MONTHS.get(month_name)
         if month is None:
             raise ValueError(f"unknown month: {month_name}")
-        return dt.date(today.year, month, day)
+        try:
+            return dt.date(today.year, month, day)
+        except ValueError:
+            # Clamp to last valid day of this month per docs.
+            import calendar
+            last = calendar.monthrange(today.year, month)[1]
+            return dt.date(today.year, month, last)
review_targets: the try/except is correct but loses information about WHY we clamped — log at INFO level?
no test for the clamping was added — needs one
